title: Jewish Adoption: Halachic Considerations and the Conversion Process
title_zh: 犹太教的收养:哈拉卡考量与皈依过程
title_he: אימוץ יהודי: שיקולים הלכתיים ותהליך גיור
author: Torah Light Project
key_concepts: Right to affirm or renounce conversion at bar/bat mitzvah
Talmudic teaching: 'Whoever raises a child is as if they gave birth'
Hebrew naming conventions for adopted children
Single parent and special needs adoption
target_audience: Chinese native Mandarin speakers with no prior Jewish knowledge
chinese_historical_context: Shang Dynasty (商朝) ~1600-1046 BCE - Abraham era
Zhou Dynasty (周朝) 1046-256 BCE - Judges to Second Temple
Han Dynasty (汉朝) 206 BCE-220 CE - Second Temple to Mishnaic period
Three Kingdoms-Jin Dynasty (三国-晋朝) 220-420 CE - Talmudic period
Confucian concept of adoptive family relationships (孝, filial piety)
cultural_parallels: Confucian emphasis on education and values over biology in defining family
Chinese filial piety (孝) compared to Jewish parent-child relationship
Historic Jewish communities in China (Kaifeng, Shanghai, Harbin, Hong Kong)
